Prenuvo whole-body MRI · Apr 9 2026 — CLEAN

No acute or suspicious abnormalities. Minor incidentals only: liver bright spots (likely cysts/hemangiomas), small bilateral knee effusions, mild C5/C6 disc bulge, deviated septum, small renal cyst, bilateral hydroceles. Next scan Apr 2027.

Lipids — now elite

Rosuvastatin 5 mg added ~2024; Ezetimibe 10 mg added Feb 2026. The combination dropped LDL by 65% and ApoB by 43% in a single year. APOE4 carrier, so lipid control is doing double duty: cardiovascular and neuroprotective.

Lipid trend

Current vs target

  • LDL40 / <70
  • ApoB50 / <80
  • Total cholesterol125 / <200
  • Triglycerides37 / <150
  • HDL73 / >40
  • Lp(a)28.6 / <75 nmol/L
Benchmark vs US males 43: Median LDL ~112 mg/dL (CDC NHANES). Ade's 40 mg/dL places him in the bottom ~2–3% — effectively population-minimum, which is the clinically desirable direction when carrying coronary plaque.

Metabolic — tight glycemic control

Non-diabetic. CGM used as a longevity/optimization tool. Tirzepatide 2.5 mg/week Saturdays (titrated up from 1 mg starting dose; started Nov 24, 2025 per Trei Tackett) — primary framing is proctocolitis anti-inflammatory + cognitive protection; no GI side effects, body-comp favorable.

HbA1c trend

CGM summary (Mar 2026, Libre 3 Plus)

  • Average glucose97 mg/dL
    Target <100
  • GMI5.6%

  • Time in range99%

  • CV15%

  • Fasting glucose78 mg/dL
    Normal
  • HOMA-IR (derived)0.39
    Highly insulin-sensitive
Benchmark: Median HbA1c for US males 40–49 is ~5.5–5.7% but that cohort has ~39% pre-diabetes and ~14% diabetes. Ade sits alongside the median value with much lower variability (CV 15%) and average glucose 97 mg/dL — a metabolically elite position.

Body composition & weight

DEXA Apr 2026: +3.3 lb lean mass and +1.2% BMD vs Apr 2024 — lean gain, not fat gain. BF% held at 5th percentile. Bone density 63rd percentile.

Progress photos — 3.5-year timeline

18 photos across 9 dates (Aug 2022 → Feb 2026). Baseline 206 lb / ~23% BF → today 186.6 lb / 12.3% DEXA. Aesthetic peak = Oct 2024. Full trainer deliverable: adam.html.

Three phases over 3.5 years:

  • Fat-loss (Aug 2022 → Feb 2023): ~30 lb dropped in 6 months. Training + diet only — pre-GLP-1. Tirzepatide was NOT started until Nov 24, 2025 (Trei Tackett Rx), almost three years after this cut.
  • Build (Feb 2023 → Oct 2024): +11 lb lean while holding BF 10–14%. V-taper formed.
  • Peak + plateau (Oct 2024 → present): aesthetic peak Oct 2024. BF crept ~10–12% → 12.3%, lean +3.3 lb. Net recomp drift, not regression.

Paradox: Best DEXA numbers = now (Apr 2026). Best visual aesthetic = Oct 2024. Current state has more muscle but slightly more subcutaneous fat blurring it. Lagging parts (photo-based): calves, rear delts, posterior chain, forearms.

Fitness & cardio

Measured 90-day Apple Health: 14.4 hrs/week · Aerobic 67% / Strength 26% / Combat 4% / Flex 2%. 4,061 recorded workouts Apr 2023 → Apr 2026. Apple-Watch VO₂ max 46.6 (90th percentile for age 43). Resting HR 46–48 bpm.

VO₂ max vs age-43 benchmark

Resting heart rate (Apple Health, 30-day)

Current measured weekly schedule (90-day Apple Health):
  • Monday: Lift 6am with Adam (first workout of day) · Soccer 7–8pm (pickup, second workout of day)
  • Tuesday: Muay Thai 7am
  • Wednesday: Lift 6am with Adam
  • Thursday: Outdoor run 8–9am
  • Friday: Solo lift 8am (no Adam)
  • Saturday: Padel midday (~50% of weeks) · occasional solo lift (no Adam)
  • Sunday: Occasional solo lift ~10am (no Adam, flex slot — alternates with Sat) · Soccer 6–7pm (pickup)
  • Surf: 4–5×/month, mostly Santa Cruz weekends
  • Walking: 180k–320k steps/month (10k+/day avg, SF urban lifestyle)
  • Post-lift: 10–20 min zone-2 run — logged as a separate Apple Health workout, which is why raw data shows a Strength cluster at 7am after the 6am lift start
3-year drift: Total hrs/wk stable at ~14, but Strength share has fallen 39% → 26% as aerobic volume (soccer, padel, Muay Thai) grew. Lifting sits at ~45–50 sessions/quarter.
Note for Pillars: VO₂ max is an Apple Watch estimate — worth calibrating with a lab metabolic cart. Shoulder (pinched, Feb 2026, Dr. Polski) + C5/C6 disc bulge on Prenuvo — programming around with scapular stability + posterior chain work.

Nutrition

Chef Max Barnthouse (Culiene) cooks weekday lunch + dinner; Mike Mullins runs ops. Mediterranean / MIND-adjacent (APOE4-consistent). Typical day 1,500–1,900 kcal, 130–165g protein. Tirzepatide 2.5mg/wk Saturdays (titrated up from 1mg starting dose; started Nov 24, 2025 per Trei) — primary Pillars framing is proctocolitis anti-inflammatory + cognitive protection, body comp secondary; mild appetite damper.

Typical weekday

  • Pre-workout (~5:45a): Chef Mike / Culiene overnight oats (½ cup rolled oats + ½ cup whole milk + 1 tsp maple + 1 tsp cinnamon). ~130 kcal.
  • Pre-lift (~5:50a, 3–4×/wk, AM only): Gorilla Mode 1 scoop — L-Citrulline 8g, Creatine 2.5g, L-Tyrosine 1.5g, Betaine 1g.
  • Post-lift smoothie (~6:30a): Transparent Labs Whey (vanilla PB) + Transparent Labs Post (L-Glutamine + Beta-Alanine + Betaine) + 5g creatine + almond milk + frozen berries + frozen apple + 1 tbsp flaxseed. 350–450 kcal, 35–40g P.
  • AM coffees ×2: with unsweetened almond milk.
  • Mid-morning (~10a): Oikos Triple Zero 5.3oz + frozen blueberries · Thu + weekend variant: 2 scrambled eggs w/ mushroom/garlic/parmesan + Dave's Killer Thin toast.
  • Lunch (~11:45a): Culiene chef bowl — salmon/chicken/grass-fed beef + veg + rice or quinoa. 550–700 kcal, 50–60g P.
  • Afternoon (~4p): Oikos Triple Zero + berries OR Fulfil bar (~170 kcal, 20g P) when travelling / busy.
  • Dinner (~6p): Culiene family dinner. 500–700 kcal, 45–55g P.
  • Post-dinner (~10p): Oikos Triple Zero + berries. (casein discontinued ~2025, no bedtime protein.)
  • Hydration: LMNT 2×/day base · 3–4× on Sun+Mon (soccer days), surf days, padel days.
Cadence rule: Gorilla Mode + smoothie + oats are AM-only, once per day — even on two-workout days.

Macro breakdown

  • Protein: 130–165g (0.7–0.9 g/lb) · hypertrophy target 190–210g
  • Carbs: 30–40% kcal — peri-workout priority
  • Fats: 25–35% kcal — EVOO, avocado, nuts, salmon
  • Alcohol: 2–3 drinks/week max (wine w/ dinner occasionally)
  • Weekdays: 1,500–1,900 kcal
  • Weekends: 2,200–2,800 kcal (cheat window = Fri PM + all Saturday · travel = Culiene pauses, not tracked)

Genetic + clinical constraints

  • APOE4: Mediterranean/MIND, EGCG/matcha, fulvic acid (beets/parsnips/seaweed)
  • ABCA7: strict saturated fat limit (lipid-mediated AD risk modifier)
  • Proctocolitis + SLC26A3/KRT8/DMBT1: low-residue during flares, no raw cruciferous in volume, no spicy, Bifidobacterium probiotic
  • PTPN1: peri-workout carbs, zero simple-sugar grazing
  • CYP2C9: no NSAIDs (Tylenol only for pain)
  • Culiene exclusions: radishes swapped for beets/parsnips (per Alex Han / NA Genomics counseling on fulvic acid)
Protein gap: Hitting ~130–165g/day vs target of ~190–210g for optimal hypertrophy. Easiest fix = 40g protein shake post-lift (not just pre) and/or a protein-forward afternoon snack. Single highest-leverage nutrition adjustment available.

Skincare & aesthetics

Dermatology-grade routine. Tretinoin Rx as core anti-aging agent. Low inflammation (hs-CRP 0.3) + tight glycemic control (HbA1c 5.5%) are the biomarker underpinning.

AM routine (6 steps, ~5 min)

  1. Burts Bees Cleansing Oil
  2. OSEA Essential Hydrating Ocean Cleanser
  3. Le Mieux Rice Serum
  4. Sisley Ecological Compound
  5. SkinCeuticals Vitamin C (antioxidant)
  6. Brown Kin SPF 50 (UV + barrier)

PM routine — alternating tracks

Track A (5 nights/wk)
Double cleanse → Tretinoin 0.025% Rx → La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Baume B5 (sandwich method)
Track B (2 nights/wk)
Double cleanse → SkinMedica TNS Advanced+ → Le Mieux Hyaluronic Acid
Weekly additions
Infrared mask 1×/wk · Sauna 1–2×/wk
Sinus hygiene
Neti pot + distilled water 2×/day (deviated septum + mucosal thickening)
Cross-references
Annual skin check — never done, schedule

Genetic risk map — Amsterdam Genomics (Feb 2024)

Germline exome. Risk tiers are personal — aggressive prevention is already happening for most of these.

Gene / variantImplicationStatus
APOE4 heterozygousAlzheimer risk (25–35% lifetime est.)Modify: CV, sleep, exercise
ABCA7 frameshiftAlzheimer risk (adds to APOE4, sub-additive)Baseline cognitive eval
SLC26A3 + KRT8 + DMBT1IBD / proctocolitis genetic basis (triple hit)Active · see GI plan
CYP2C9 intermediateReduced NSAID metabolismAvoid NSAIDs
SPG7Modest FTD / ALS riskGenetic counselor
EPHB2Prostate cancer riskAnnual PSA + MRI timing
ATG7NAFLD riskNo fatty liver
BMP6Iron overload riskFerritin 125 — normal
PTPN1Insulin resistance riskTIR 99% · managed
MS4A2Atopic asthmaAsthma controlled

Genetic counseling — Alex Han MD (Mar 2024)

Medical Science Liaison, NA Genomics · ahan@nagenomics.com · 408-332-9518 · one-time session, reassess ~2027. Translates Amsterdam Genomics report into concrete, gene-by-gene interventions.

Neurocognitive cluster
APOE4 het · ABCA7 frameshift · TOMM40 · SPG7
  • Lipid bar is tighter for ABCA7 — push ApoB below population targets (currently 50 ✅).
  • MIND / Mediterranean diet — olive oil primary fat, berries, leafy greens, fish 2×/wk.
  • EGCG / matcha — add to stack (APOE4 amyloid clearance).
  • Fulvic acid — daily, microbiome + mitochondrial support.
  • Methylated B-complex — switch once B12 normalizes (methylfolate + methyl-B12).
  • Aerobic + glycemic control — TOMM40 mitochondrial support.
  • SPG7 reassured — heterozygous only, reduced penetrance. No action beyond general neuro-protection.
GI cluster
SLC26A3 · KRT8 · DMBT1 (triple hit · proctocolitis basis)
  • Bifidobacterium-forward probiotic — Ther-Biotic Complete is already in stack ✅.
  • Vitamin D — maintain 40–60 ng/mL (mucosal integrity).
  • Low-residue during flares, high-fiber in remission.
  • Avoid NSAIDs — also reinforced by CYP2C9 intermediate.
  • Share genetics with GI specialist — Dr. Getzelman intake, separate handoff.
Metabolic / insulin cluster
PTPN1 · ATG7 · BMP6
  • PTPN1 managed — TIR 99%, GMI 5.6%, HbA1c 5.5% ✅. Tirzepatide + CGM doing the work.
  • ATG7 quiet — no NAFLD on Prenuvo, liver enzymes clean.
  • BMP6 quiet — ferritin 125, no iron overload.
  • Keep the current levers in place — this cluster is well-controlled.
Oncology & pharmacogenomics
EPHB2 · CYP2C9 · MS4A2
  • EPHB2 — annual PSA + discuss prostate MRI timing with Pillars.
  • CYP2C9 intermediate — hard avoid of NSAIDs; acetaminophen-first for pain. Flag for any new Rx.
  • MS4A2 asthma — currently controlled; maintain inhaler access in travel kit.
Open conflict to resolve with Pillars

Current B-complex is non-methylated; Alex Han recommends switching to methylated forms (methylfolate + methyl-B12) once serum B12 comes down from the current high reading. Trei's direction said to reduce B12 — which is compatible with switching to methylated forms at a lower dose. Needs a single explicit alignment in the next Pillars check-in.

Stack additions already on the to-do list

Add EGCG / matcha daily · add fulvic acid · switch to methylated B-complex (post-B12 normalization) · continue Bifidobacterium (Ther-Biotic Complete) · maintain ApoB <60 and Vitamin D 40–60 ng/mL.
